content.text:
Now I have a clear 3D view of the molecule with all atom indices labeled. Let me examine atom C:21 (carbon, index 21) more carefully.

From this detailed view, I can clearly see that atom C:21 is connected to:
- C:14 (carbon) - to the left
- C:22 (carbon) - to the upper right
- C:26 (carbon) - to the lower right
- O:5 (oxygen) - directly above C:21 (the red sphere)

I missed the oxygen atom O:5 in my previous analysis. Looking at the bonds, C:21 has four connections: three carbon atoms (C:14, C:22, C:26) and one oxygen atom (O:5).

The oxygen atom O:5 appears to be part of a carbonyl group or similar functional group attached to C:21.

So the complete list of neighbor atoms for C:21 are:
- Atom index 14, element C
- Atom index 22, element C
- Atom index 26, element C
- Atom index 5, element O

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 5819 (from the filename "geomconf_5819.xyz").

Let me confirm once more by checking if there are any hydrogen atoms directly bonded to C:21. From the current view, I don't see any hydrogen atoms directly attached to C:21 - the hydrogens are attached to other carbon atoms in the structure.

Therefore, the neighbor atoms of atom index 21 (C) are: C:14, C:22, C:26, and O:5.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 21, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
